pi_items.source: Consistently referenced in task.json description (line 5), setup_task.sh line 41, export_result.sh line 17, and verified through verifier.py result JSON structure which expects this file location.

Metadata claim found in task.json line 18. However, verifier.py does not check this constraint. export_result.sh line 26 captures report_size but verifier.py pass condition (line 162) only checks: passed = score >= 60 and openice_running. No validation of report_size >= 150 exists in the verification logic.

Task description (task.json lines 3-4) explicitly requires measurement of 'Heap memory usage', 'CPU usage percentage', and 'Thread count'. verifier.py lines 116-143 validate these metrics are present in report content with regex patterns for memory (heap|memory|vmrss|resident|usage with MB/GB units), CPU (cpu|processor with % values), and thread (thread keyword with numeric values).

Task description (task.json line 2) explicitly states: 'create 2 simulated device adapters of DIFFERENT types'. verifier.py lines 69-78 checks for at least 2 devices created and awards 20 points if len(created_devices) >= 2.

Confirmed real platform. install_openice.sh line 4 and setup_openice.sh demonstrate OpenICE is the real open-source medical device integration platform from MD PnP Program (GitHub: https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp). Official docs: https://www.openice.info/. Installed with Java 17 (install_openice.sh line 14) which aligns with OpenICE 2.0 Java 17 requirements.

Task description (task.json line 3) mentions using 'jstat, top, ps, jmap, or /proc' to measure Java process performance. These are real Java diagnostic tools verified as standard JDK tools via Oracle Java SE documentation and industry standard performance monitoring tools.

pi_items.metadata_value: Establish performance baseline for OpenICE platform under load
pi_items.verified_value: Establish performance baseline for OpenICE platform under load
pi_items.source: Task description (task.json) establishes the clinical/operational context: measuring heap memory, CPU usage, and thread count of OpenICE Java process while running 2 simulated medical device adapters to establish a performance baseline.

privileged_info_summary: The runtime_metrics_baseline task requires using OpenICE (https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp), a real open-source medical device integration platform developed by the MD PnP Program, to measure JVM performance metrics. The task requires creating 2 simulated medical device adapters of different types, then measuring the OpenICE Java process's heap memory usage, CPU percentage, and thread count using standard Linux/Java tools (jstat, jmap, top, ps, /proc), and documenting findings in a report at /home/ga/Desktop/performance_baseline.txt. The required metrics are memory, CPU, and thread count. All claimed device types and diagnostic tools are verified as real. The data is synthetic (simulated medical device data). IMPORTANT: The metadata claim of min_report_size: 150 bytes is not enforced by the verifier.py - the pass condition only checks score >= 60 and OpenICE running status.
